subject: 📊 Daily Portfolio: +2.8% | TSM past TP1 · NVDA recovered · UNCY PDUFA in 7 days
html_body:

Sources: stockanalysis.com (NVDA $210.69 +2.95% Jun 18) · Yahoo Finance (LLY $1,098.57 −1.19% Jun 18) · stockanalysis.com (AVGO $411.35 Jun 18) · Investing.com/Kraken (TSM $462.12 +6.94% Jun 18).

📋 ACHV Update — CRL Received (manufacturing issue)

ACHV received a Complete Response Letter (CRL) around its June 20 PDUFA date — FDA cited manufacturing issues at a third-party contract manufacturer, not efficacy or safety. The drug works; the factory doesn't pass inspection yet. Achieve has partnered with Adare Pharma Solutions for US-based manufacturing. NDA resubmission: Q4 2026. Potential approval: H1 2027. Stock likely cratered on CRL news. Not a trade until Q4 2026 resubmission at earliest. Remove from radar for now.

⚡ Exit Strategy Review

🔔 TSM — TP1 PASSED. Trim now.

TSM closed $462.12 on June 18 (+6.94% that session). TP1 was $435 — the stock is now +$27.12 above TP1. This is a new 52-week high ($465.22 intraday on Jun 18). Analyst consensus average is $465–$473 — you're inside that range now. Limited remaining analyst-target upside before the stock needs a new narrative.

Stop: $357 (prior breakout base — far below, fully protected ✅)  |  TP1: $435 (PASSED ✓)  |  TP2: $485 (+5.0% from here)  |  52-wk high: $465.22

Trim plan (sell 50% = 0.112 shares):

Sell: 0.112 sh × $462.12 = $51.76 gross proceeds
Cost basis on shares sold: 0.112 × $388.35 = $43.49
Gross P&L: +$8.27 | Exit commission: −$3.00 | Net P&L: +$5.27 ✅
Remaining: 0.112 sh → targets TP2 at $485 (+5% more upside)

Why now: At analyst consensus, holding for TP2 is +5% more upside. Trimming books a clean +19% win on half, stops you from watching a winner turn flat. The $48.76 freed can fund UNCY (see below) with PDUFA in 7 days. Your call.

⚠️ LLY — $15 from TP1 ($1,114). Commission math: barely clears.

Close $1,098.57. TP1 $1,114 = only 1.4% away. ATH was $1,160.95 on Jun 11. Stock pulled back ~5.3% from ATH and is recovering. Jefferies target $1,350 (raised). Orforglipron launch ongoing.

Commission check at TP1: (1114−994.87) × 0.02958 sh = +$3.52 gross P&L | −$3.00 commission | Net: +$0.52. Barely profitable. Recommendation: don't trim here. Wait for $1,160+ (prior ATH) where the math improves significantly. Set alert at $1,110. Hold.

⚠️ NVDA — Recovered. Stop 3.2% away. Thesis intact.

Important update from last report: NVDA was at $204.65 (Jun 17, flagged as near-stop 🚨). On Jun 18 it surged +2.95% to $210.69 — reclaiming ground and buying breathing room. Stop at $204 is now 3.2% below, not 0.3%. The 50-day MA sits at ~$206–$209, right below current price; NVDA is holding above it.

Stop: $204 (50-day MA support cluster) — 3.2% below | Key resistance: $211–$215 zone (must clear to re-test highs) | TP1: $240 | TP2: $260 | Next catalyst: Q2 FY27 earnings ~Aug 2026

Watch VWAP behavior today (see education section). If NVDA holds above intraday VWAP through 2 PM ET, institutions are buying the dip. If it keeps reversing at VWAP from below, distribution is underway and the $204 stop is at risk. No action — let the market structure confirm direction first.

✅ AVGO — Confirmed at $411.35. Recovery thesis intact. Ex-div today.

Previous report estimated ~$407. Confirmed Jun 18 close: $411.35 (+1.1% vs estimate). Post-earnings selloff (−15% on Jun 5 from Hock Tan not raising FY guidance) bottomed at $386.62 and has since recovered +6.4% to $411. Jun 22 is ex-dividend ($0.65) — stock opens ~$0.65 lower today in morning quotes; not meaningful given position size.

Stop: $379 (below Jun 5 post-earnings low at $386.62 — 7.9% away ✅) | 50-day MA: ~$422 (overhead resistance, stock below it) | TP1: $474 | TP2: $536 | Next catalyst: Q3 FY26 earnings ~Sep 2026

⏳ Pending Watchlist — Waiting on IBKR Approval

VRDNViridian Therapeutics⏳ WATCH · 6/10

🔒 IBKR application pending

~$16.60  vs entry target $20.00 — price 17% below target

⚠️ Entry conditions changed — re-analyze before entering if IBKR approves

VRDN dropped from $20 target to ~$16.60 (-17%). Stop at $14.50 is only $2.10 away (12.7% risk from current). Market may be pricing lower probability than 82%. Run fresh levels analysis before any entry.

Stop: $14.50 | TP1: $28 | TP2: $37 | Analyst avg target: $34.18

🟡 SOON — 8 DAYS

Veligrotug for Thyroid Eye Disease · PDUFA June 30, 2026

Priority Review · Breakthrough Therapy Designation · BLA accepted · Company launch-ready commercially.

Approval prob: 82%  |  Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED

🚨 8 days to PDUFA. IBKR must approve before June 28 to trade the event. If not approved: monitor outcome for potential post-approval entry (very different risk profile).

VERAVera Therapeutics🟢 BUY NOW · 7/10

🔒 IBKR application pending

$34.26  vs entry target $34.04 — ✅ Entry window open (+0.6%)

Stop: $24.50 | TP1: $55 (50%) | TP2: $78 (50%)

🟢 15 DAYS

Atacicept for IgA Nephropathy · PDUFA July 7, 2026

BTD + Priority Review · 46% proteinuria reduction (p<0.0001) · Zero IgAN CRLs in history · Director Enright $5.28M open-market buy · Analyst avg target $78 (+127% upside)

🆕 June 2 FDA Update: Earlier eGFR confirmatory analysis pulled to Q3 2026

Original timeline: eGFR analysis in 2027. New: FDA and Vera aligned on Q3 2026 analysis → supplemental BLA Q4 2026 → potential FULL approval 2027. The wait for full approval just compressed from 2+ years to 6 months post-PDUFA. Thesis significantly strengthened since June 10 entry.

Approval prob: 89%  |  Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED — check Musaffa (pre-revenue biotech, debt structure)

IBKR approval is the only obstacle. When approved: enter $204 at ~$34.26, stop $24.50. This is the priority deployment target for current cash. Every IgAN PDUFA so far: zero CRLs. BTD + p<0.0001 + director conviction buy.

📡 Catalyst Plays — June 22, 2026

UNCYUnicycive Therapeutics🔴 URGENT — 7 DAYS

Catalyst: FDA NDA decision — Oxylanthanum Carbonate (OLC) for hyperphosphatemia in CKD dialysis patients  ·  PDUFA June 29, 2026

Class II NDA resubmission after first CRL. FDA raised no concerns on clinical, safety, or preclinical data — the first CRL was likely CMC/manufacturing (similar to ACHV situation). Three clinical studies supporting NDA. Commercial preparations for potential Q3 2026 launch underway. Cash: $54.9M runway into 2027.

Market cap

~$182M ✅

Short interest

~8.56%

Price (~Jun 18)

~$7.12

Cash runway

Into 2027 ✅

Approval probability: ~55% — Class II resubmission with FDA citing no clinical/safety issues. Base rate for this profile: ~60–70%. Discounted for prior CRL track record and unknown CRL root cause. No insider buys. CEO publicly optimistic. Analyst consensus Strong Buy, avg target $25.67.

✅ If APPROVED

~$14–$18 (+97–153%)

Analyst consensus $25.67 on approval; first OLC-based phosphate binder

💀 If 2nd CRL

~$2.00–$3.00 (−58–72%)

Cash floor ~$1.63/sh; second CRL is typically a severe draw-down

EV on 28 shares ($199): 0.55 × +$193 − 0.45 × −$120 = +$52 expected (positive, but high variance — could lose $120)

Smart money: No insider buys found. Analyst consensus Buy. Launch preparations underway (positive signal). Pre-score: 4/5.

Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED — CKD treatment (mineral metabolism); likely compliant but check Musaffa before entering. Pre-revenue, check debt structure.

🔴 INVESTIGATE · 4/5

7 days to PDUFA. Check Musaffa today. If halal clears: consider 28 shares (~$199) using current cash. If TSM trim clears first ($48.76), you can go up to 35 shares (~$249 = 30% position cap). Say 'analyze UNCY' for full 8-section deep dive. Your call.

🔬 MLTX — MoonLake Immunotherapeutics SKIP ❌

Phase 3 VELA Week 52 data released Jun 21 + Investor Day today (8 AM ET). Strong data: 67.2% HiSCR75 at Week 52. BLA submission planned Sept 2026. Market cap $1.37B. Pre-score 4/5. Fails halal: Musaffa classifies MLTX as NOT HALAL as of Feb 2026. End of analysis. Skip.

🔬 DFTX — Definium Therapeutics (formerly MindMed) SKIP ❌

Three Phase 3 readouts in 2026 — Emerge MDD data expected late Q2 (could be any day). Well-funded ($411M cash). Pre-score 4/5. Fails halal: MM120 is LSD (lysergide d-tartrate), a psychedelic / mind-altering substance. AAOIFI prohibition applies. Fascinating science. Wrong universe.

💵 Cash Deployment — $204.81 deployable

Cash: $213.81 | Less $6 reserve | Less $3 buy commission = $204.81 into position | Plus TSM trim if executed (+$48.76) → $253.57 deployable

Option A — UNCY (7 days, 55% probability, halal pending):

28 shares × $7.12 = $199.36 + $3 commission = $202.36. Stays within budget. Position = 31.6% portfolio (slightly over 30% cap — size down to 25 shares = $178 for clean sizing). EV: +$52 expected. Risk: −$120 if second CRL. Halal must clear first — check Musaffa today.

Option B — Hold for VERA (15 days, 89% probability, broker blocked):

VERA is higher conviction (89% vs 55%) but broker-blocked. If IBKR approves in time: $204 into VERA at ~$34.26 = ~6 shares. Combined with TSM proceeds: ~7 shares ($245). Better EV, higher conviction.

⭐ Recommended: A then B — or B only

Check UNCY halal today. If halal clears AND you want to trade both events: use current cash for UNCY now ($199), use TSM trim proceeds ($48.76) to add to VERA when IBKR approves. If halal doesn't clear: hold everything for VERA. Don't force UNCY. Your call.

🔄 Rotation — TSM TP1 → UNCY + VERA

Suggested: Sell 0.112 TSM shares at market open today (~$462). Gross $51.76 | Net after −$3: +$48.76 freed (+$5.27 net P&L). Then: deploy into UNCY now (if halal clears, $199) and into VERA when IBKR approves ($245 combined with TSM proceeds). This sequences two binary catalyst plays over the next 2 weeks.

Risk: TSM could rip to TP2 ($485) before you sell — but you keep 0.112 shares for that ride. The trim is right: you're inside analyst consensus already, the easy money is made. Your call.

📚 Day 24 of 30

Volume Profile and VWAP

VWAP — volume-weighted average price — is the benchmark every institutional desk uses to judge their own executions. It weights each price level by the volume that traded there during the session. The result: a single line that tells you where most money changed hands. Institutions buying try to fill below VWAP; institutions selling try to fill above it. Today's NVDA recovery from $204.65 to $210.69 on June 18 — was that real buying or a dead-cat? Check where NVDA spent most of June 18 relative to VWAP. If it held above VWAP all afternoon, that +2.95% close reflects genuine demand. If it kept rejecting at VWAP from below, that's distribution dressed as a recovery. AVGO's $386.62 post-earnings low on June 5 — that's a high-volume support node on the volume profile, which is why the $379 stop sits below it: if $386 holds with heavy volume, that's real support; if it breaks on high volume, $379 is the next logical floor.

For your UNCY and VERA pre-event trades: watch for low-volume drift vs high-volume directional moves in the week ahead. If either stock starts accumulating (rising on above-average volume while holding above VWAP) before the PDUFA, that's a smart money signal; if it drifts higher on thin volume, that's retail — much weaker hand.

Also today: Anchored VWAP — start the VWAP from a specific event date (like AVGO's Jun 3 earnings or TSM's Jun 18 surge). This anchored line shows exactly where late buyers have their average cost — a key support/resistance level that daily VWAP misses entirely.

Prices as of Thu June 18, 2026 close (last session before Juneteenth federal holiday + weekend). Markets reopen today.
